page: The Vultr vhp-1c-2gb-intel is a virtual machine instance belonging to the High Performance server family. It is equipped with a single shared vCPU based on the Intel Xeon Cascadelake architecture running at a clock speed of 2.0 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 2.0 GB of system memory and a local storage allocation of 50 GB NVMe SSD. No dedicated GPU resources are included.

Benchmark evaluations indicate entry-level performance characteristics. The single-core stress-ng CPU performance score is 1365.08, and the Passmark CPU score is 1669.57, both placing in the lower percentiles. Memory bandwidth is measured at 96.65 GB/sec for small cached blocks and drops to 8.8 GB/sec for large uncached blocks. Single-threaded bzip3 decompression shows moderate capability at 9.03 GB/sec, while Redis SET operations reach 1.56 million operations per second.

This instance offers a highly cost-efficient entry point for lightweight workloads, leveraging shared CPU resources to minimize resource overhead. The primary tradeoff is the shared CPU allocation and limited memory capacity, which constrains its ability to handle concurrent multi-threaded tasks or memory-intensive applications.

Given the hardware profile and benchmark results, this server is best suited for low-traffic static web serving, basic development environments, lightweight background tasks, and small-scale testing. It is not recommended for database hosting, large-scale LLM inference, or high-throughput production applications.
description: The Vultr vhp-1c-2gb-intel is an entry-level virtual server from the High Performance family, featuring a single shared Intel Xeon Cascadelake vCPU running at 2.0 GHz. It is configured with 2.0 GB of RAM and 50 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, with no GPU acceleration. Benchmark results place this instance in the lower performance tiers, with a Passmark CPU score of 1669.57 and a single-core stress-ng score of 1365.08. Memory bandwidth is limited, reaching 8.8 GB/sec for large uncached blocks. This resource profile offers a highly cost-efficient option for lightweight, non-critical workloads. The shared CPU allocation and modest memory capacity make it suitable for basic development environments, low-traffic static web serving, and simple background processes, while rendering it unsuitable for resource-intensive database operations or multi-threaded production applications.
og_description: Vultr vhp-1c-2gb-intel is a budget-friendly virtual server featuring a single shared Intel Xeon Cascadelake vCPU, 2.0 GB of RAM, and 50 GB of NVMe SSD storage, ideal for lightweight development tasks.

tagline: A budget-friendly, shared-core virtual server with NVMe storage designed for basic development, testing, and low-traffic static web hosting.
